Title: Devotional: Jeff Bednar
Description:
Jeff Bednar is an associate professor in the Management Department at BYU. His research focuses on how leaders construct and maintain their identities, and how they respond to various identity challenges, such as legacy dynamics and the impostor phenomenon.

In addition to research, Bednar teaches courses related to organizational behavior and human resource management, and works with organizations and executives wrestling with issues related to identity, culture and leadership transitions. He has a bachelor’s and master’s degree in accounting from BYU as well as a PhD in management and organizations from University of Michigan.

His remarks will be broadcast live on BYUtv, BYUtv.org. Video, text, and audio are archived on speeches.byu.edu.

Title: Ask Me Anything about Finding God and Yourself in Your Career Path
Description:
Joey Leavitt is a Foreign Service Officer (FSO), former intelligence analyst, and serious music aficionado. As a consular-coned FSO, Joey has run overseas operations for immigrant visas, non-immigrant visas, and American citizen services in El Salvador, Abu Dhabi, and India. He also served as a political and economic officer in Saudi Arabia and a State Department liaison with the intelligence community during a tour in Washington, DC. Joey studied Arabic for a year in Syria on his way to a BA in Middle East Studies/Arabic from BYU, and he also holds a law degree. He’s dad to three adult children and one high schooler, husband to a project manager in the tech industry, and loves trail running, reading, photography, and thinking about 80’s power ballads. Joey is currently studying Chinese in preparation for a tour in Taiwan.

Sponsored by the Kennedy Center for International Studies.

Title: Devotional: Jamal Willis
Description:
Jamal Willis is the assistant director of Student Wellness at BYU. His work experience consists of a former high school and middle school administrator, counselor and school district athletic administrator. Jamal holds a master’s degree in education administration, and a master’s in educational counseling.

Willis grew up in Las Vegas, Nevada and joined the Church at the age of 21. He played four years at BYU and became the all-time leading rusher and went on to play in the NFL for the San Francisco 49ers.

His remarks will be broadcast live on BYUtv, BYUtv.org. Video, text, and audio are archived on speeches.byu.edu.

Title: GIS Faculty Workshop
Description:
Discover how interactive maps and spatial thinking can support teaching, research, storytelling, and student engagement across disciplines. Taught by the BYU Library Geospatial Services, this hands-on workshop series introduces approachable, web-based GIS tools in ArcGIS Online, including StoryMaps, dashboards, data collection apps, and simple spatial analysis techniques. Participants will explore practical ways to incorporate maps, geographic data, and interactive web tools into courses, assignments, research, and public scholarship.


No previous GIS experience is required. All workshops are independent from each other, and participants are welcome to attend any session based on their interests and availability. Faculty, staff, and graduate students from all disciplines are invited to join. All workshops will be held in HBLL 2233.



Register here: https://survey123.arcgis.com/share/22cf810f97f046b6b0644c04093c7f76

Title: Summer Carnival
Description:
Get ready for an afternoon of summer fun at the BYU Summer Carnival! BYU Employee Wellness invites all BYU faculty, staff, administration, and their families to join them for a free event designed to celebrate the summer season and bring the BYU community together.

Enjoy a variety of activities for all ages, including lawn games, carnival games, friendly races, delicious snacks, and opportunities to win prizes throughout the event.

They will be hosting a talent show, and participants of all ages are invited to share their talents! Whether you sing, dance, play an instrument, perform a unique skill, or have another talent you'd like to showcase, they'd love to see it. Those interested in performing can sign up using the link on their website.

The Summer Carnival is a chance to relax, have fun, and create lasting memories with the people who make BYU such a special place. Bring your family, invite your coworkers, and come celebrate summer with Employee Wellness.

**Don't forget to bring sunscreen and water! **

A completed waiver is required for all participants before taking part in the event. Please ensure that every attendee has the appropriate waiver completed, including children.

Title: Devotional: Rebecca Hunter
Description:
Rebecca Hunter is an associate teaching professor in the Department of Teacher Education at BYU. After more than a decade as a public-school educator, she earned a doctorate in Educational Leadership and Policy from the University of Utah. She also holds a master’s degree in public administration from BYU, and a bachelor’s degree in elementary education from the University of Utah.

Along with teaching courses in social studies methods and assessment, her research focuses on the clinical experiences of preservice teachers and processes of teacher change.

Her remarks will be broadcast live on BYUtv, BYUtv.org. Video, text, and audio are archived on speeches.byu.edu.

August 6, 7:30 p.m.

Title: Classic Film: Sunset Boulevard, 1950
Description:
The series ends the summer with a Hollywood classic.

Sunset Boulevard tells the story of a down on his luck screenwriter who is drawn into the dark world of a faded film star.

Admission is free.

Title: Devotional: Elder Patrick Kearon
Description:
Elder Patrick Kearon was sustained as a member of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in April 2024. He previously served as Senior President of the Seventy Presidency and member of the Europe Area Presidency.

Elder Kearon has lived and worked in the United Kingdom, Saudi Arabia, and the United States in a range of industries and ran his own communication consultancy. His civic and community involvement has included service on the boards of charities, a school, an enterprise agency, and a college.

His remarks will be broadcast live on BYUtv, BYUtv.org. Video, text, and audio are archived on speeches.byu.edu.

TICKETS ON SALE JULY 15!

Title: Utah Chamber Music Festival
Description:
Musicians from the United States, Germany, Georgia, Russia, and the Netherlands come together for a performance of some of the greatest pieces of chamber music ever written. Each performer is an internationally renowned soloist in their own right, and this performance will be a unique opportunity to hear this all-star group of musicians perform together at BYU for the first time.

Title: BYU STEM Career Fair
Description: What job opportunities can stem from STEM? Find out at BYU’s STEM Career Fair, held in the Ballroom of the Wilkinson Student Center.

Title: Student Event: Starship Library
Description:
For one night only, the library will be transformed into a vast and exhilarating galaxy! In this sci-fi themed event, you can take the role of a space cadet in training to become a space ranger. Stay for as little or as long as you want to visit different planets, play games, complete quests and challenges, and earn stellar prizes.

Come find your space in the library!

The event begins on the main floor of the library

Admission is FREE!

Space-themed costumes are welcome!

Title: Steep Canyon Rangers
Description:
Americana band Steep Canyon Rangers kick off the BRAVO! Series’ celebration of America’s musical heritage with their Grammy-winning blend of traditional bluegrass forms and contemporary songwriting. Hailing from North Carolina, the Rangers incorporate regional storytelling and literary influence into their craft and have long occupied a singular space in American roots music.

September 17, 7:30 p.m.
CONCERT HALL (MB)
$12-47

Title: Classic Film: King Kong, 1933
Description:
The original 1933 King Kong is an early monster film.

The library has the papers of both the director, Merian C. Cooper, and the score composer, Max Steiner.

Critics called it "thrilling" when was released and it was re-released in various versions over the years before being restored. The library of Congress declared it "culturally, historically and aesthetically significant" and added it to the National Film Registry.

This film is often mentioned when people look at the way 1930's Hollywood portrayed the world.

Admission is free.

Title: Stephen Wilkes: America at 250
Description:
Experience five decades of American history through photography. Fine art photographer and speaker Stephen Wilkes, best known for his groundbreaking Day to Night images, presents a talk that celebrates America’s 250th birthday. Capturing fifty years of America’s people, landscapes, and culture, America at 250 offers a powerful visual reflection on change, resilience, and the moments that define us.

September 30, 7:30 p.m.

Title: All the Beautiful Things
Description:
Drawn directly from the words of real people, this world-premiere play tells the stories of individuals from diverse religious backgrounds who wrestle with doubt, then rediscover their connection to God. This work invites audiences to recognize the moments in their own lives when they have felt God’s love, reminding them that God is mindful of all His children.

Title: Fabio Menchetti, Piano: From Bach to Ligeti
Description:
Fabio Menchetti, associate professor of piano at Washington State University, will perform solo piano works from the 18th and 20th century, connecting two eras far from each other but also subtly connected. Starting with an English Suite by Bach, the program will culminate with Musica ricercata by Ligeti, to commemorate the 20th anniversary of the composer’s death.

October 21, 7:30 p.m.
